messages.content:
Those who looked up at the right time on July 9, 1926, would have witnessed an annular solar
eclipse, during which the Moon blocks most of the Sun and causes it to look like a ring of
light.______stargazers on December 24, 1927, would have seen a partial solar eclipse, during
which the Moon only blocks a portion of the Sun and causes it to look like a crescent.
Which choice completes the text with the most logical transition?
a) A) As a result,
c) C) Simultaneously,
b) B) On the other hand,
d) D) Indeed,

The most logical transition for this text, which moves from discussing one astronomical event to a different one occurring on a different date, is choice B) "On the other hand,". This choice effectively indicates a shift or contrast between two separate events, which is appropriate given the change from describing an annular solar eclipse to a partial solar eclipse at different times.

Portable video game consoles and wireless speakers tend to contain batteries that can't be easily
____. Environmental policy researcher Jessika Richter warns that because new batteries can't be
put in once the old ones no longer work, the gadgets stop functioning and are usually disposed of
as trash.
Which choice completes the text with the most logical and precise word or phrase?
a) A) rated
c) C) prevented
b) B) invented
d) D) replaced

The most logical and precise word to complete the text is:

d) D) replaced

This choice directly addresses the issue described by Jessika Richter, regarding the challenge of devices becoming unusable and disposed of as waste due to their batteries not being easily replaceable.

Domesticated thousands of years ago by Indigenous people in South America, the potato deviates
structurally from the wild plant it is descended from. Summer squash, another crop domesticated
by Indigenous Americans, shows so little resemblance to any wild plant that genetic research was
necessary to ____ the wild Johnny ground as its ancestor.
Which choice completes the text with the most logical and precise word or phrase?
a) A) confirm
c) C) develop
b) B) acquire
d) D) attract

The most logical and precise word to complete the text is:

a) A) confirm

This choice accurately conveys the idea that genetic research was needed to definitively identify or verify the wild counterpart or ancestor of the domesticated summer squash.

The following text is from Mark Twain's 1876 novel The Adventures of Tom Sawyer.
Tom, a child. has been told by his aunt to paint their house's fence. Tom appeared on the sidewalk
with a bucket of whitewash and a long handled brush. He surveyed the fence, and all gladness lef
him and a deep melancholy settled down upon his spirit. Thirty yards of board fence nine feet high.
As used in the text, what does the word "surveyed" most nearly mean?
a) A. Looked at
c) C. Had questions about
b) B. Ordered
d) D. Was captivated by

As used in the text, the word "surveyed" most nearly means:

a) A. Looked at

When considering which plays and musicals to produce, theaters in San Francisco often favor
keeping audiences happy over taking risks. So while they might be eager to produce an
established classic like Amadeus, for example, most would be______ to stage a work from a
relatively unknown playwright.
Which choice completes the text with the most logical and precise word or phrase?
a) A) determined
c) C) fortunate
b) B) hesitant
d) D) surprised

The most logical and precise word to complete the text is:

b) B) hesitant

This choice accurately reflects the cautious approach theaters might take towards producing works by relatively unknown playwrights compared to established classics, due to a preference for keeping audiences happy and avoiding risks.

President Richard Nixon is most famous for his partcipaton in the 1970s Watergate politcal scandal,
a convoluted tale of criminality and eroded ethics involving a constellaton of associates such as
Chief of Staf H.R. "Bob" Haldeman and former CIA ofcer James W. McCord. But Nixon's legacy is
complex: he has been praised for his role in opening relatons between the United States and
China, and he once made an atempt at reforming United States health care policy that is arguably
a precursor to the Afordable Care Act, which became law during the Barack Obama administraton.
The text makes which point about Richard Nixon?
a) A. He publicly regreted his connectons to
H.R. "Bob" Haldeman and James W.McCord.
c) C. He is most widely known for his connecton
to the Watergate scandal.
b) B. He implemented changes to United States
health care policy that lasted untl the
presidency of Barack Obama.
d) D. He has been wrongly critcized for the
causes he supported.

c) C. He is most widely known for his connection to the Watergate scandal. 

The text highlights Nixon's involvement in the Watergate scandal as a significant aspect of his legacy, alongside mentioning his efforts in other areas like opening relations with China and health care policy attempts.

Known for the albums Quiet Nights and Milestones, jazz trumpeter Miles Davis collaborated several
times with pianist Gil Evans. Their 1958 adaptation of George Gershwin's opera Porgy and Bess
bears little resemblance to the 1935 original. Davis and Evans felt no desire to please listeners
expecting an exact duplication of the opera. They omitted parts, such as the aria "I Got Plenty of
Nuthin'," and sometimes made only brief gestures toward Gershwin's melodies. But Davis and
Evans's willingness to recompose Gershwin's work led to one of the most enduring albums in
Davis's catalog.
According to the text, what is true about the aria "I Got Plenty of Nuthin' "?
a) A) It appears in the Gershwin opera Porgy
and Bess but not in the adaptation by Davis
and Evans.
c) C) Listeners familiar with Gershwin's body of
work generally disliked it.
b) B) A brief quotation from it appears in Davis
and Evans's adaptation of Porgy and Bess
d) D) It was adapted by Davis in a recording that
appears on his album Milestones.
a) A) It appears in the Gershwin opera Porgy and Bess but not in the adaptation by Davis and Evans.

In 2019, 20 previously unknown moons were conﬁrmed to be orbiting Saturn. Three of the moons
have prograde orbits (orbiting in the direction the planet spins), and the other 17 have retrograde
orbits (orbiting in the opposite direction of theplanet’s spin). All but one of the 20 moons are thought
to be remnants of bodies that orbited Saturn until they broke apart in collisions. Although the one
exceptional moon orbits in the same direction as the planet’s spin, its orbit is highly eccentric
compared to the rest, which may suggest that it has a diﬀerent origin than the other 19 moons.
Based on the text, which choice best describes the moon with the eccentric orbit?
a) A. It doesn’t have a retrograde orbit, but it
likely has the same origin as the moons with
retrograde orbits.
c) C. It has a prograde orbit that is likely the
result of having collided with another body
orbiting Saturn.
b) B. Its orbit is so tilted with respect to the other
moons’ orbits that it’s neither prograde nor
retrograde.
d) It has a prograde orbit and may not be a
remnant of an earlier body that orbited
Saturn.
d) It has a prograde orbit and may not be a remnant of an earlier body that orbited Saturn.

Having written the impassioned call to arms “Letter to the Spanish Americans” in 1791, Peruvian
intellectual Juan Pablo Viscardo y Guzmán is often considered a forerunner for the independence
movements in Latin America. But Viscardo’s role in history would have remained insigniﬁcant were
it not for Venezuelan revolutionary Francisco de Miranda, who was handed the unpublished letter
after Viscardo’s death. Miranda not only helped circulate the letter, but his edits and footnotes to the
text position Miranda as a central ﬁgure in the text’s creation.
Which choice best states the main idea of the text?
a) A. The original authorship of “Letter to the
Spanish Americans” is disputed by
contemporary historians.
c) C. Miranda played a crucial role in inﬂuencing
the content and distribution of “Letter to the
Spanish Americans.”
b) B. The majority of the most eloquently stated
arguments in “Letter to the Spanish
Americans” were written by Miranda.
d) D. “Letter to the Spanish Americans”
persuaded many people in Latin America to
pursue national independence.
c) C. Miranda played a crucial role in influencing the content and distribution of “Letter to the Spanish Americans.”

In the South Paciﬁc, New Caledonian crows use two diﬀerent kinds of stick tools. One tool is
complex. The crows shape a stick from a rare plant into a hook. The other tool is basic. The crows
ﬁnd a stick without a hook on the ground. The hooked tool is harder to get but is much better than
the basic tool at removing prey from holes. When studying New Caledonian crows, ecologist
Barbara Klump found that they hold the hooked tools in their claws when not using them, or they
carefully put them in a safe place. The crows don’t do the same with the basic tools. This suggests
to Klump that the______
Which choice most logically completes the text?
a) A. hooked stick tools are more valuable to
the crows than the stick tools without hooks.
c) C. crows prefer to share their hooked stick
tools but don’t share the stick tools without
hooks.
b) B. hooked stick tools are easier for most of
the crows to hold than the stick tools without
hooks.
d) crows realize that both kinds of stick tools are
less eﬀective than their claws are at
removing prey from holes.
a) A. hooked stick tools are more valuable to the crows than the stick tools without hooks.

North American gray wolves usually have mixed gray and white fur, but some members of the
species have a version of a gene, or gene variant, that gives them a mostly black coat instead.
Sarah Cubaynes and her team studied twelve populations of North American gray wolves. They
found that the black-furred wolves are more common in areas where outbreaks of distemper virus
happen regularly. The team also discovered that the black-furred wolves are more likely to be
immune to distemper than the gray-furred wolves are. Taken together, these ﬁndings suggest that
______
Which choice most logically completes the text?
a) A. North American gray wolves experience
more outbreaks of distemper virus than other
wolf species do.
c) C. the average life span of gray wolves is
likely to increase over time because of a
particular gene variant.
b) B. the gene variant that results in black fur
may be linked to immunity to the distemper
virus.
d) D. gray-furred wolves will soon replace black-
furred wolves across North America.
b) B. the gene variant that results in black fur may be linked to immunity to the distemper virus.

The Bronze Age in Britain lasted from around 2500 BCE to approximately 700 BCE. Collections of
metal items (called hoards) from all periods of the Bronze Age have been found in Britain, including
the Llanarmon-yn-Iâl hoard of artifacts from the 16 century BCE, unearthed around 1982, and the
much later Horsehope Craig hoard of artifacts from the 7 century BCE, unearthed around 1865.
Sometime in the Middle Bronze Age (approximately from the 14 century BCE to the 10th century
BCE), metalsmiths in Britain began to develop swords for the ﬁrst time. This fact helps explain why,
whereas evidence of sword production was found in_________________
Which choice most logically completes the texts?
a) A. both the Llanarmon-yn-Iâl hoard and the
Horsehope Craig hoard, only the latter
included evidence of more reﬁned sword
production.
c) C. the Llanarmon-yn-Iâl hoard, no intact
swords were found among the items in the
hoard.
b) B. hoards discovered before 1865, no such
evidence was found in hoards that were
discovered later.
d) D. the Horsehope Craig hoard, no such
evidence was found in the Llanarmon-yn-Iâl
hoard.
d) D. the Horsehope Craig hoard, no such evidence was found in the Llanarmon-yn-Iâl hoard.

he following text is adapted from Jerome K. Jerome's 1889 novel three Men in a Boat ( To
say Nothing of the Dog).
We [people] are creatures of the sun. We love light and life. That is why we crowd into the towns
and cities, and the country grows more and more deserted every year. In the sunlight - in the
daytime, when Nature is alive and busy all around us, we like the open hill-sides and the deep
woods well enough: but in the night, when our Mother Earth has gone to sleep, and left us waking,
oh! the world seems so lonesome, and we get frightened, like children in a silent house. Then we
sit and sob, and long for the gas-lit streets, and the sound of human voices, and the answering
throb of human life.
Which choice best states the main purpose of the text?
a) A) To indicate that people sometimes
experience feelings of isolation in crowded
areas
c) C) To caution people not to be so quick to
dismiss the natural beauty that can be found
in rural areas
b) B) To address common traits that motivate
many people to choose to live in urban
environments
d) D) To demonstrate that most people tend to
prefer being alone in the country to being
alone in towns or cities
b) B) To address common traits that motivate many people to choose to live in urban environments

When classical pianist Martha Argerich performs, it appears as if the music is coming to her
spontaneously. She's highly skilled technically, but because of how freely she plays and her
willingness to take risks, she seems relaxed and natural. Her apparent ease, however, is due to a
tremendous amount of preparation. Despite Argerich's experience and virtuosity, she never takes
for granted that she knows a piece of music. Instead, she approaches the music as if encountering
it for the first time and tries to understand it anew.
Which choice best states the main purpose of the text?
a) A) To provide details about how Argerich
identifies which pieces of music she will
perform
c) C) To discuss the kinds of music Argerich
feels most comfortable encountering for the
first time
b) B) To assert that Argerich's performances
look effortless because of how she prepares
for them
d) D) To describe the unique way that Argerich
approaches music she hasn't performed
before
b) B) To assert that Argerich's performances look effortless because of how she prepares for them

Dubautia waialealae is a species in a family of plants known collectively as the silversword alliance,
all of which grow only on the Hawaiian Islands. Members of this alliance exhibit an extraordinary
range of phenotypes, with some species maturing into vines and others into shrubs and trees. All
species in the alliance descended from a single ancestral tarweed plant that arrived on the islands
around 5 million years ago. The tarweed's descendants diversified into distinct species as they
adapted to live in the wide variety of habitats found on the Hawaiian Islands.
Which choice best describes the function of the underlined phrase(known collectively as the silversword alliance,) in the text as a whole?
a) A) It lists species from a family of plants
found only on the Hawaiian Islands.
c) C) It explains why Dubautia waialealae is
unique among members of the silversword
alliance.
b) B) It provides the common name for the
Dubautia waialealae plant.
d) D) It supplies the name used to refer to a
group of related plant species.
d) D) It supplies the name used to refer to a group of related plant species.

The size of bird eggs varies widely across species, and the researchers, _____ computer imaging to
compare the lengths of eggs from species like Buteo albicaudatus and Treron vernans, aimed to
shed light on the evolutionary basis for such variation.
Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of standard English?
a) A) to use
c) C) used
b) B) using
d) D) use
b) B) using

The Tripitaka Koreana is a collection of over 80,000 woodblocks. Created in the 13th century, it is the
oldest and most comprehensive version of the Buddhist canon, with no known errors or typos in its
volumes. Each woodblock was made through an elaborate ________ was soaked in sea water for
three years, then boiled in salt water, then exposed to wind for three years, then covered in a
poisonous lacquer to keep insects from damaging the wood, and finally framed with metal to prevent
the wood from warping. This elaborate process created woodblocks that are in pristine condition
despite being over 750 years old.
Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
a) A. process: birch wood
c) C. process—birch wood:
b) B. process. Birch wood,
d) D. process birch wood
b) B. process. Birch wood,
